sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Intersect governance coordination, technical stewardship, incident response, release coordination, repository management, security initiatives, ecosystem coordination, and management of critical processes for June 2026 to June 2027.
Recipient: Intersect via the 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v

Deliverables: WP 1 - Intersect operations and ecosystem coordination: 6,000,000 ADA, WP 2 - Technical Stewardship, Incident Response & Coordination including core Cardano repos: 18,800,000 ADA, WP 3 - Management of critical processes: 600,000 ADA, Use of treasury management smart contracts developed by Sundae Labs with TRSC and PSSC structure, Milestone-based drawdowns, evidence-backed reporting, transparent disclosures, and external assurance

Claim (other, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Intersect states that this proposal funds its core operating model for June 2026 to June 2027, including governance coordination, critical unowned processes, technical stewardship, incident response, and release coordination.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 25,400,000 ADA, allocated as 6,000,000 ADA for operations and ecosystem coordination, 18,800,000 ADA for technical stewardship and incident response, and 600,000 ADA for management of critical processes.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Intersect asserts that its overall ask has reduced from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the functions most critical to Cardano continuity and maturity.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Intersect asserts it has coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal says it achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and was advanced for on-chain Treasury Withdrawal submission under the approved Budget Process Framework.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal claims the requested amount does not, at submission, individually or in aggregate breach the 350M Net Change Limit covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713.
missing: Independent evidence for: Intersect asserts that its overall ask has reduced from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the functions most critical to Cardano continuity and maturity.
Independent evidence for: Intersect asserts it has coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.

Independent evidence for: Independent audits and assurance for this proposal are stated to be provided by Appold, with possible reviews including deliverable verification, control testing, spend validation, and assessment of milestone evidence.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Intersect states that this proposal funds its core operating model for June 2026 to June 2027, including governance coordination, critical unowned processes, technical stewardship, incident response, and release coordination." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Intersect asserts that its overall ask has reduced from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the functions most critical to Cardano continuity and maturity." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
